title: oxalis intermedia
intro: Oxalis intermedia, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalis genus and is native to South America. The medicinal properties of Oxalis intermedia include its ability to act as a diuretic, reducing inflammation and improving urinary function. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of kidney stones, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Oxalis intermedia is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
